summary: 2026-07-10 09:36:47 PDT — The branch is mostly aligned with the docs-consistency sweep: BUG-1/OPS-1 are tracked, the main README/server/dev-doc multiplier references are removal-only, security.html is reachable, and the operator-decryptable disclosure is present. I found two remaining public-doc consistency issues plus one date NIT.

findings.title: Security card reintroduces client-only wording
findings.claim: The new Security index card says the page covers "what never leaves the Zoom client." That is too broad after this sweep: the same docs now correctly state that names/opportunity-cost values are sent to the server, stored encrypted at rest, and operator-decryptable. This public entry point can read like the old browser-only privacy claim the sweep is meant to retire.
findings.suggestion: Change the card description to narrower wording, e.g. "encryption, headers, and aggregate-only overlay data" or "what participants never see."

findings.title: Default-rate tip is still per-person-only
findings.claim: The page now presents Simple mode as the default and per-person mode as host/co-host-only, but this tip still tells all users to "Set a sensible default so people you have not listed are still counted." Non-hosts are Simple-locked and do not manage listed people/default rates, so the public docs are not fully reconciled with the Simple-default + host-only per-participant model.
findings.suggestion: Scope the tip to per-person mode, e.g. "If you use per-person mode, set a sensible default so unlisted attendees are still counted," or replace it with a Simple-mode average/count tip.

findings.title: Index effective date stayed stale
findings.claim: This branch adds the Security nav link and Security card to the docs index, but the footer still says "Effective date: 1 June 2026" while the other touched public docs were bumped to 9 July 2026. That leaves the date-consistency NIT only partially applied.
findings.suggestion: Bump the index footer to 9 July 2026, or remove the effective-date footer from the index if the portal page is not intended to be dated.
